config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Kalau kepentingan umum selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan atas nama kebaikan bersama. Bagi saya, keputusan yang adil harus mencari keseimbangan, bukan mengorbankan hak pribadi begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not fully accept the statement, because in my view society and the individual should be balanced rather than ranked absolutely. Coming from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability and public order, since social harmony and long-term development matter a lot, and sometimes individual preferences do need to give way. But if “society first” becomes a blank check, it can easily excuse ignoring fairness, due process, and basic personal dignity, which I do not think is acceptable. A strong society should protect individual rights precisely because that is what keeps collective power from going too far.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak seharusnya otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, padahal justru hak individu dibutuhkan agar mayoritas atau penguasa tidak sewenang-wenang. Memang benar stabilitas dan ketertiban penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar dikorbankan terlalu mudah, yang lahir bukan masyarakat kuat melainkan ketidakadilan yang dibungkus retorika bersama. Bagi saya, ukuran yang sehat adalah keseimbangan yang jelas: kepentingan publik boleh dibatasi, tetapi hak individu tidak boleh dihapus begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society ahead of the individual as a general rule, because that formula is too easy to abuse. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, and I do not dismiss those concerns at all, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can become a convenient excuse to silence ordinary people or skip fair procedures. A society is not truly strong if it can only function by weakening personal dignity and basic rights. The better principle is balance: protect the public interest, but keep clear limits so individual rights are not treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering terdengar mulia, tetapi dalam praktiknya mudah dipakai untuk membungkam yang lemah atau menutup kritik, jadi saya tidak mau itu dijadikan pembenaran otomatis. Stabilitas dan ketertiban penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikorbankan seenaknya, masyarakat justru kehilangan keadilan dan kepercayaan. Buat saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang tegas: kepentingan publik boleh dibela, tetapi hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because society should not automatically outrank the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of stability, order, and collective goals, but I also know how easily “public interest” can be used to justify silencing ordinary people or ignoring fair procedures. A society that weakens personal dignity and basic rights in the name of unity is not truly strong; it is only more efficient at control. The right balance is to protect the public good while treating individual rights as real limits, not as disposable obstacles.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
